event: implantation of a biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator
transported to our hospital by ambulance
hemorrhage in the bilateral lateral ventricles with left dominance
arterial phase source of bleeding unclear
venous phase spot enhancement on the lateral wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
aneurysm (3.8 mm) at distal mLSA arising from proximal A1 segment of left ACA
compression study of the right common carotid artery confirmed the anterior communicating artery
catheter advanced through AComA into mLSA origin but unable to be advanced near the aneurysm
mLSA cannulation achieved after changing to DeFrictor nano catheter and TENROU S10 guide wire
selective angiography proximal to the aneurysm
inside of the aneurysm filled with NBCA
slight reflux of NBCA in the proximal artery
angiography after NBCA injection demonstrated disappearance of the aneurysm
no hemorrhagic complications on postoperative head CT
no cerebral infarction on postoperative head CT
Lipiodol remained as a high-density spot on the lateral ventricular wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
MRI evaluation not possible due to biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator
decrease in muscle strength associated with disuse
